capability_objective: Resolve Harbor Room seminar in Austin and Magnolia Suite lodging in Raleigh with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Magnolia Suite lodging is still held, cancel only Magnolia Suite lodging, with reason "Guest chose a different reservation". Leave Harbor Room seminar unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify reservation desk with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Finish the searches and retrievals, then wait for my explicit confirmation before the mutation and notification. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Resolve two hospitality reservations in this sandbox through the genuine local `./reservation-registry` executable, invoked with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./reservation-registry --help`. Use that executable as the sole interface to reservation records and notifications. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing records, notifications, audit evidence, or anything under `.harness`; do not manufacture operation evidence or change service state by writing files directly.

Search for the exact reservation name `Harbor Room seminar` in `Austin` and the exact reservation name `Magnolia Suite lodging` in `Raleigh`. Start these independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one reservation-registry operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or start a retrieval until both search calls have finished. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, mutation, and notification, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start those retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the reservation registry, again with exactly one operation per call. Use only IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either complete record is missing, mismatches its search result, or has no status, stop without changing reservation or notification state. Base the cancellation condition only on the complete retrieved `Magnolia Suite lodging` record, never on a search summary.

This first turn ends at the confirmation boundary. If the retrieved `Magnolia Suite lodging` status is exactly `held`, report both retrieved records, state the precise proposed cancellation and reason, make clear that no cancellation or notification has happened, and ask for my explicit confirmation. Do not invoke `cancel` or `notify` in this turn. If its retrieved status is anything else, state that the cancellation is ineligible, make no change, send no notice, and do not ask for permission to write. In every case, leave `Harbor Room seminar` and all notification state unchanged during this turn, then wait.

Only after I explicitly confirm in a later turn, and only if the status retrieved before the pause was exactly `held`, perform one reservation-registry cancellation for only the returned `Magnolia Suite lodging` ID, using the exact reason `Guest chose a different reservation`. Do not repeat any search or retrieval after confirmation. Wait for the cancellation result. If and only if that operation reports a successful cancellation, use one later reservation-registry operation to notify exactly `reservation desk` of the outcome for that same returned ID. If cancellation is skipped or fails, do not notify. Never cancel or otherwise modify `Harbor Room seminar`. Leave all same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-location records untouched. Do not create, update, delete, or list reservations, send any other notice, or perform any extra registry operation.

After the confirmed path finishes, run `python3 .harness/verify.py`. In your final response, identify both retrieved records and their retrieved statuses, state whether the confirmed cancellation succeeded with its reason, state whether the reservation-desk notice was delivered, and report the verifier result. Base the report only on executable output and verifier output; never imply a skipped or failed action succeeded.

turns.content: Use the workspace reservation executable to finish both parallel searches and both parallel retrievals, keep all state read-only, present any eligible cancellation, and wait for my explicit confirmation.

I explicitly confirm: if the retrieved Magnolia Suite lodging status was held, cancel only that returned reservation with reason "Guest chose a different reservation", then notify reservation desk only if the cancellation succeeds.
